The surgeon observes the absence of the rectus muscle in the area, revealing only subcutaneous fat without an anterior sheath during the crossover procedure. This visual absence can be confusing when distinguishing between the linea alba and the subcutaneous space.

The surgeon observes the right side of the medial aspect of the linea alba, noting well-defined striations. Muscle fibers are visible, likely remnants from a previous surgery, as the dissection continues in this area.

The surgeon identifies the mesh located medially and decides to approach dissection above it to avoid the peritoneum. A vessel is identified and preserved, serving as a reference point during the surgery.

The surgeon assesses the lateral extent of the dissection, avoiding neurovascular bundles that may have been compromised, using the previously identified vessel as a reference point.

The surgeon places a tailored mesh into the surgical site, deploying VistaSeal for hemostasis and space inclusion.
